**Interpretation of Data**

### **Duration Codes**

*   S = Single Semester
*   Y = Full Academic Year

### **Result Codes**

*   A = Absent
*   AO = Registered for Attendance Only
*   C = Pass by Compensation
*   D = Deferred Result
*   E = Exemption
*   F = Fail
*   FN = Fail; Time Owing on Clinical Practice
*   FG = Fail Competence
*   FB = Fail Attendance
*   I = Certified Illness
*   L = Laboratory Pass Only
*   P = Pass
*   PF = Passed Link Module
*   T = Theory Pass Only
*   W = Failed a Coursework Element
*   X = Failed an Examination Element
*   S = Resit Assessment Element as per module information
*   R = Resit Exam Element

### **ECTS Grades**

*   A = Excellent: outstanding performance with only minor errors
*   B = Very Good: above average performance but with some errors
*   C = Good: generally sound work with some notable errors
*   D = Satisfactory: fair but with significant shortcomings
*   E = Sufficient: minimum criteria met
*   FX = Fail: some more work required
*   F = Fail: considerable further work required

### **DCU/ECTS Credits**

*   A student attending full-time at the University will normally accumulate 60 ECTS (European Credit Transfer System) credits per year. A 3-year degree programme would have a total ECTS credit value of 180 and, similarly, a 4-year degree programme would have a value of 240 ECTS credits.

**Assessment Methodology**

*   In general, student performance is determined at the conclusion of each academic year. In taught programmes, this is based on a combination of attainment in end-of-semester examinations (both semesters) and continuous assessment of academic exercises carried out throughout the academic year. Research programmes of study are assessed on the basis of a submitted thesis and oral examination; although in the case of Masters Degrees the latter may be waived at the discretion of the examiners.

**Pass Mark**

*   The minimum mark required for a pass in a module is 40% unless stated otherwise in programme regulations. A candidate who fails to attain the required pass standard in all modules taken at a single sitting may, at the discretion of the Progression and Award Boards, be allowed to pass the module as a whole by compensation, subject to the approval of Academic Council.

**Exemptions**

*   In general, all modules passed by a candidate in a particular year of study should have such passes held in perpetuity unless removed as a consequence of a subsequent disciplinary decision approved by Academic Council.

**Award Classifications: Primary Degrees**

| Level of Award | Pre Academic Year 2007/08 | Academic Year 2007/08 onwards |
| :-------------- | :------------------------ | :--------------------------- |
| First Class Honours | Not less than 70% | Not less than 70% |
| Second Class Honours-Grade 1 | Not less than 63% | Not less than 60% |
| Second Class Honours-Grade 2 | Not less than 55% | Not less than 50% |
| Third Class Honours | Not less than 50% | Not less than 40% |
| Pass | Not less than 40% | N/A |

**Award Classifications: Other Awards**

### 6.1 A Certificate, Diploma, Graduate Certificate or Graduate Diploma (in most cases) may be awarded as follows:

| % Average Mark | Pre Academic Year 2021/22 | Academic Year 2021/22 onwards |
| :------------- | :------------------------ | :--------------------------- |
| Not less than 70% | Distinction | First Class Honours |
| Not less than 60% | Credit | Second Class Honours-Grade 1 |
| Not less than 50% | Pass | Second Class Honours-Grade 2 |
| Not less than 40% | Pass | Third Class Honours |

### 6.2 A Masters Degree obtained through coursework and dissertation may be awarded as follows:

| % Average Mark | Pre Academic Year 2017/18 | Academic Year 2017/18 onwards |
| :------------- | :------------------------ | :--------------------------- |
| Not less than 70% | First Class Honours | First Class Honours |
| Not less than 60% | Second Class Honours | Second Class Honours-Grade 1 |
| Not less than 50% | Pass | Second Class Honours-Grade 2 |
| Not less than 40% | Pass | Third Class Honours |

### 6.3 A Masters Degree by research is awarded without classification.

### 6.4 Doctoral Degrees are awarded without classification.
